channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.mmorpg.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.mmorpg.com

item.title: Valorborn, A Medieval Fantasy Sandbox RPG, Has Launched Into Early Access On PC Today
item.content:encoded:
It's the week of sandbox RPGs, as Valorborn launches into early access today. The Medieval Fantasy-inspired sandbox aims to bring a player-driven world to players. 

Developed by Turkish studio Laps Games, the sandbox RPG takes place in the kingdom of Thareon in the wake of a "great magical cataclysm." In the world of Valorborn, players can apparently take any role that tickles their fancy, whether it's a sellsword or a tradesman dominating the kingdom's trade routes. It looks like the aim here is to create a compelling player-driven beyond your standard sandbox experience. 

From today's press release:

"In Valorborn, the life you lead is yours to define. Become a sellsword and earn coin by solving the troubles of villagers, live off the wilderness with allies in your own encampment, produce and trade goods, hunt, fight, build, or forge a path entirely your own. There are no fixed classes. Character growth is shaped through the actions players take and the choices they make."

In the future, the plan is apparently to bring more systems, kingdoms, factions and more to Valorborn as it rides into 1.0 down the road. The basic systems are in place during this early access launch, including its progression system, exploration, trade and more. It's a survival game, so you'll be able to create your own base and encampment should you choose with you and some friends. 

You can grab Valorborn on Steam or the Epic Games Store for $24.99 starting today. The team is planning to release a roadmap and workshop support soon, and the team is planning a livestreamed Q&A next week to talk about feedback and more in the wake of today's launch.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/valorborn-a-medieval-fantasy-sandbox-rpg-has-launched-into-early-access-on-pc-today-2000137811
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Temtem: Pioneers Details What's Changing, What's Returning, As Survival Title Adopts Real-Time Combat
item.content:encoded:
Christina Gonzalez Updated: Apr 15, 2026 3:00 PM Posted: Apr 15, 2026 3:00 PM ET
Category: News 0

Combat in Temtem: Pioneers is going to have an important role, and be in real-time. Crema revealed more about the new systems that also keep the spirit of the original system, in their survival crafting title.

As of now, they know that if you’re coming from the previous Temtem franchise games, you’re likely familiar with the original Temtem taming systems and combat. In working to raise the stakes and broaden the roles your Tems will take, they also needed to make some updates and a few changes.

After weakening a wild Temtem in combat, you can then catch them. This time, though, combat is in real time. And you get to control  the Temtem in battle. No turns, no commands from outside. It’s you. You fight, and decide how to react. 

In the Downbelow, each Temtem species has one of two Traits, one that benefits the Tem itself. Each Temtem also has inherent stats that include HP, attack, speed, and defense. Single Values, which run 1-30, join with the base stat to form the final stat value.. Each creature you capture will have randomized SVs. The Pedigree System will offer a way to improve stats later. 

Temtems use combos, a baseline close-range physical attack, and Techniques in combat. Techniques (Techs) are each Temtem’s special ability. They’re usable in battle and a Tem can unlock more along the way as they level. They can use up to four. You'll also find that dashing is a valuable thing to learn.

Since combat will be in real-time,  they acknowledge that all of this is going to take some time to get used to for many people. 

As part of the overall combat package, Gears are back, to equip your Tems with for passive bonuses. This time though, you'll have to craft them yourself. Synergies also return, this time through the use of Trinkets, quest items you’ll unlock during your journey. Find the right combination and become a powerful force to use Synergies in combat.

You can read the full combat entry to get an overview. Crema will be revealing more later, but for now, they do tease additionalcrafted and consumable items that can also affect your stats, potentially even permanently, and more to think about. 

Since you’re making all of the decisions and fighting as your Temtem, this is going to be interesting.

Christina Gonzalez

Christina is MMORPG.COM’s News Editor and a contributor since 2011. Always a fan of great community and wondering if the same sort of magic that was her first guild exists anymore.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/temtem-pioneers-details-whats-changing-whats-returning-as-survival-title-adopts-real-time-combat-2000137810
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: The First Descendant Onslaught Endgame Mode Beta Begins in Tomorrow's Update,
item.content:encoded:
Christina Gonzalez Updated: Apr 15, 2026 2:25 PM Posted: Apr 15, 2026 2:00 PM ET
Category: News 0

Nexon’s The First Descendant team streamed a preview of their next update, set for tomorrow, that will add a new endgame mode, Onslaught, in a preview beta, along with some balance changes, new Modules, quality of life updates, and events.

The patch is set for tomorrow, April 16th, and Onslaught Mode, despite being released now into a two-week beta, is at the center of the update. Onslaught is a defense mission with your goal to stop the Vulgus from reaching their target point. That sounds simple, but If enough monsters reach the objective, then you fail the mission.

Defeat enemies in the oncoming waves to shore up your side by building or upgrading turrets. These can help attack enemies and even disrupt their movement to help sell them down so you can regroup to continue taking down as many enemies as possible. At the end of some waves, you'll get to pick from random ability options to help build up your kit as well

Each mission will cover across three zones, each with its own characteristics and challenges. Keep mowing down waves and eventually face a Colossus as a final enemy. 

The beta will run through April 29th, with an intended official release on May 21st alongside Season 3 Episode 4. So if you take part, the team wants feedback to improve the mode before its final release.

Tomorrow's update  has a number of balance changes, for some weapons and two Descendants,  Harris and Viessa. Changes to Harris focus on strengthening her overall performance,  with new HP-based damage, longer passive duration, and new range and more damage for Crushing Charge.

Viessa is getting reworked to improve some of her less popular transcendent modules. After the changes, she’ll still be a mid-range attacker capable of quick and powerful damage, with an added firearm-based build. 

Tomorrow's update also kicks off a number of new events, including a 6-week login event, ‘Esiemo's Onslaught Preparation Mission’, which can earn you rewards like Superconductive Cooling Material and Superheated Material.

You can hit play on the video for the developer Q&A that happened during the live stream. The team has worked on a number of improvements, but content is now clearly back on the agenda. They addressed several questions, including feedback on grindiness. They revealed that next month, we'll get us a more streamlined farming loop, and later, we’ll get a new onboarding experience and overhauled new player experience. These are just some of the changes on the way to help improve the player experience.

Christina Gonzalez

Christina is MMORPG.COM’s News Editor and a contributor since 2011. Always a fan of great community and wondering if the same sort of magic that was her first guild exists anymore.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/the-first-descendant-onslaught-endgame-mode-beta-begins-in-tomorrows-update-2000137808
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: The First Descendant Onslaught
item.content:encoded:
Christina Gonzalez Updated: Apr 15, 2026 2:25 PM Posted: Apr 15, 2026 2:00 PM ET
Category: News 0

Nexon’s The First Descendant team streamed a preview of their next update, set for tomorrow, that will add a new endgame mode, Onslaught, in a preview beta, along with some balance changes, new Modules, quality of life updates, and events.

The patch is set for tomorrow, April 16th, and Onslaught Mode, despite being released now into a two-week beta, is at the center of the update. Onslaught is a defense mission with your goal to stop the Vulgus from reaching their target point. That sounds simple, but If enough monsters reach the objective, then you fail the mission.

Defeat enemies in the oncoming waves to shore up your side by building or upgrading turrets. These can help attack enemies and even disrupt their movement to help sell them down so you can regroup to continue taking down as many enemies as possible. At the end of some waves, you'll get to pick from random ability options to help build up your kit as well

Each mission will cover across three zones, each with its own characteristics and challenges. Keep mowing down waves and eventually face a Colossus as a final enemy. 

The beta will run through April 29th, with an intended official release on May 21st alongside Season 3 Episode 4. So if you take part, the team wants feedback to improve the mode before its final release.

Tomorrow's update  has a number of balance changes, for some weapons and two Descendants,  Harris and Viessa. Changes to Harris focus on strengthening her overall performance,  with new HP-based damage, longer passive duration, and new range and more damage for Crushing Charge.

Viessa is getting reworked to improve some of her less popular transcendent modules. After the changes, she’ll still be a mid-range attacker capable of quick and powerful damage, with an added firearm-based build. 

Tomorrow's update also kicks off a number of new events, including a 6-week login event, ‘Esiemo's Onslaught Preparation Mission’, which can earn you rewards like Superconductive Cooling Material and Superheated Material.

You can hit play on the video for the developer Q&A that happened during the live stream. The team has worked on a number of improvements, but content is now clearly back on the agenda. They addressed several questions, including feedback on grindiness. They revealed that next month, we'll get us a more streamlined farming loop, and later, we’ll get a new onboarding experience and overhauled new player experience. These are just some of the changes on the way to help improve the player experience.

Christina Gonzalez

Christina is MMORPG.COM’s News Editor and a contributor since 2011. Always a fan of great community and wondering if the same sort of magic that was her first guild exists anymore.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/the-first-descendant-onslaught-2000137808
